title: cytisus grandiflorus subsp. haplophyllus
intro: Cytisus grandiflorus subsp. haplophyllus, also known as the Balkan broom, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae), characterized by its showy yellow flowers and thorny stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, derived from its flavonoid and phenolic compound content. Consuming Cytisus grandiflorus subsp. haplophyllus has been associated with various health benefits, such as reduced cardiovascular risk, improved cognitive function, and enhanced immune system function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine, although its use should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
